| APPENDICES |
|---|

## Appendix A: Production Troubleshooting Quick Reference
### Optical Fault Decision Tree

| Observation | First Check | If Confirmed | If Not Confirmed |
|---|---|---|---|
| LOS alarm + multiple channel alarms, same node receive | Span fiber continuity (OTDR or power meter) | Fiber cut — verify APS switch; dispatch field crew | Check pre-amplifier input power — suspect in-line component failure |
| OSNR declining, span loss stable | EDFA pump current vs. nominal | Pump degradation — schedule replacement within 7 days or emergency based on margin | Check connector cleanliness; check EDFA NF history |
| OSNR stable, BER elevated | DSP DGD estimate; check CD compensation | PMD event or CD mismatch — adjust compensation; investigate fiber PMD | Check transponder card temperature; check receive power level |
| Intermittent BER spikes, no LOS | Correlate with temperature logs, protection switch logs | Thermal: check card temp CLI; Physical: check loose connector | Check DGD estimate during spike events; consider fiber PMD exceedance |
| NMS alarm, CLI shows clear | Force NMS alarm resync from NE | NMS stale state — resync and confirm; log as false alarm | Check NMS threshold configuration; verify CLI output is current-interval data |
| Wavelength provisioned in NMS but no optical power | Check WSS routing table in NE CLI | NMS-NE sync failure — force resync; verify WSS route installed | Check ROADM WSS port health; check upstream transponder TX power |
| Latency increase, no optical alarms | NMS routing path comparison: current vs. baseline | Route change — restore original path; investigate triggering event | Check ROADM switching latency modes; check fiber dispersion profile |

## Appendix B: CLI Command Reference Card
### By Task — Ciena 6500 / WaveLogic

| Task | Ciena 6500 Command |
|---|---|
| Check port TX/RX power | show port optical <port-id> |
| Check channel PM (current 15-min) | show channel <ch-id> pm current 15min |
| Check amplifier status | show amplifier <amp-id> status |
| Show active alarms (optical filter) | show alarm active   filter optical |
| Show protection status | show protection status <circuit-id> |
| Force protection switch | protection force-switch <circuit-id> |
| Show coherent modem DSP parameters | show channel <ch-id> dsp-status |
| Show equipment card status | show equipment <slot-id> |
| Show system status | show system status |
| Alarm sync (NMS-NE resync trigger) | alarm-sync force <node-id> |

### Nokia 1830 PSS

| Task | Nokia 1830 PSS Command |
|---|---|
| Check optical channel PM | display optical-channel <ch> performance-monitoring current |
| Check amplifier detailed status | display amplifier <amp> detailed-status |
| Check channel alarm list | display optical-channel <ch> alarm-list |
| Check span attenuation | display span <span-id> attenuation |
| Check pump status and history | display amplifier <amp> pump-status |
| Check WSS port routing | display wss <node> port-routing channel <ch> |
| Force NMS resync | resync-ne <node-id> (OneControl NMS CLI) |
| Show active alarms | display alarm active all |

### Cisco NCS 1004

| Task | Cisco NCS 1004 Command |
|---|---|
| DWDM port overview | show controllers dwdm 0/0/0/0 |
| OTS port status | show controllers ots 0/0/0/0 |
| All amplifiers summary | show optical amplifier all |
| Coherent DSP parameters | show controllers coherent-driver 0/0/0/0 |
| Active alarms by location | show alarms active location 0/0/CPU0 |
| Show PM history | show controllers dwdm 0/0/0/0 pm history |

## Appendix C: Alarm Correlation Matrix
### Top 20 Root Causes and Symptom Patterns

| # | Root Cause | Primary Alarms | Secondary / Symptomatic Alarms | Distinguishing Characteristic |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Fiber cut (complete) | LOS on working span RX; OPWR-FAIL on all channels at receive node | OTN-AIS propagation; client-side LOS/AIS; NMS topology DOWN | All channels on affected span fail simultaneously at same timestamp |
| 2 | EDFA pump complete failure | AMPLIFIER-FAIL; INPUT-POWER-FAIL at downstream node | All channels OSNR-DEGRADED or OPWR-LOW downstream; OTN-AIS | All channels affected on downstream spans; gain = 0 |
| 3 | EDFA single pump degradation | PUMP-DEGRADED; GAIN-DEVIATION; GAIN-TILT-EXCEED | Subset of channels OSNR-WARN; upper or lower band channels may show OPWR-HIGH | Gain tilt — different channels affected differently; not all fail |
| 4 | Fiber partial cut / high IL splice | Span LOSS-HIGH; OSNR-DEGRADED on all channels of affected span | Pre-FEC BER elevated on all channels; Q-factor decline | All channels degraded equally (no tilt); OTDR shows discrete event |
| 5 | Connector contamination | OPWR-LOW on specific channels; OSNR-WARN | Pre-FEC BER elevated; may be intermittent if vibration-sensitive | Often affects one or few channels; location-specific; OTDR shows back-reflection |
| 6 | WSS port failure / drift | Channel OPWR-LOW or OPWR-HIGH on specific add/drop channels | OSNR-WARN; channel power outlier in PM data | Only add/drop channels affected; through channels unaffected |
| 7 | Transponder card failure (hard) | EQUIPMENT-FAIL; TX-POWER-FAIL on specific wavelength | LOS at far-end receive; OTN-AIS propagated from near-end | Single wavelength; TX power drops to zero or maximum |
| 8 | Transponder thermal degradation | CARD-TEMP-HIGH (if TCA set); BER-ELEVATED; Q-WARN | Intermittent pre-FEC BER spikes; no LOS | Correlates with rack/room temperature; self-recovers as temperature drops |
| 9 | PMD exceedance (DGD spike) | DGD-THRESHOLD-EXCEED; DSP-LOCK-LOSS (brief) | Pre-FEC BER spike; customer traffic interruption 200–400 ms | CD estimate stable; DGD elevated; weather correlation (wind/temperature) |
| 10 | CD out-of-range | DSP-LOCK-LOSS; CD-OUT-OF-RANGE; PRE-FEC-BER-EXCEED | Complete traffic loss until DSP re-acquires; may take seconds to minutes | CD estimate shows large deviation; may follow fiber routing change |
| 11 | NMS stale alarm state | NMS: CRITICAL alarm; CLI: no alarm; power meter: normal | None (service is healthy) | Service not impacted; CLI contradicts NMS; last NE sync time is recent |
| 12 | NMS-NE database sync failure | NMS: circuit ACTIVE; NE: channel not optically present | Customer circuit not appearing at far end | NMS shows provisioned; WSS routing table missing entry; sync time is stale |
| 13 | APS hold-off timer misconfiguration | APS switch time >50 ms; customer traffic interruption beyond 50 ms | SLA breach notification; protection switch recorded in event log | Protection eventually switches but exceeds ITU-T G.873.1 target |
| 14 | Optical routing change (latency) | No optical alarms; customer latency SLA breach notification | NMS route display may not update if routing change is path-level only | OSNR healthy; latency measurement elevated; NMS routing audit reveals extra hop |
| 15 | OSC failure (site) | OSC-FAIL; NE-UNREACHABLE in NMS (for that span) | NMS loses visibility to downstream NEs via OSC management chain | Traffic wavelengths unaffected; management plane blind to downstream |
| 16 | Power supply failure (node) | PSU-FAIL; NODE-POWER-DEGRADED | Multiple cards may enter fault state simultaneously; some protection switches | Multiple equipment alarms simultaneously; node-wide impact |
| 17 | ROADM degree failure | WSS-FAIL; multiple channel OPWR alarms on affected degree | All channels on that ROADM degree lose power; APS switches for protected circuits | Affects all channels on one ring direction; adjacent degrees unaffected |
| 18 | Submarine cable PMD increase (seismic) | DGD-EXCEED on multiple fiber pairs; BER-ELEVATED on 100G+ circuits | Capacity degradation; some circuits fall back to lower modulation | Correlated with seismic event; affects multiple adjacent repeaters; CD stable |
| 19 | HVAC failure (equipment room) | Multiple CARD-TEMP-HIGH alarms; BER-ELEVATED on thermally sensitive cards | Cascading card failures if temperature continues to rise; protection switches | Multiple nodes/cards simultaneously; temperature log correlation is definitive |
| 20 | Firmware defect (vendor SW bug) | Unexpected card reset; EQUIPMENT-COMM-FAIL; intermittent alarm storms | Variable — depends on defect; may mimic other root causes | Multiple identical cards affected simultaneously; vendor advisory correlation; not correlated with physical events |

## Appendix D: MOP Template Library
### Template 1 — Amplifier Card Replacement (Protected Network)
Use for: Any EDFA, pre-amp, booster, or ILA card replacement on a network with 1+1 OLP protection. Modify steps 3–6 for specific vendor CLI syntax.
### Template 2 — Wavelength Reprovisioning (ROADM Network)
Use for: Any change to an existing wavelength's optical path through a ROADM node. Includes: WSS routing change, channel frequency change, power level adjustment.
### Template 3 — Firmware / Software Upgrade (NE)
Use for: Any planned software or firmware upgrade on a production network element. Includes mandatory backup step, rollback procedure, and verification sequence.
### Template 4 — Span Extension (New Fiber Section)
Use for: Adding a new fiber section to an existing DWDM route. Covers OTDR verification, OSC provisioning, amplifier optimization, and acceptance testing in sequence.
### Template 5 — Protection Path Validation (Annual Test)
Use for: Annual mandatory protection switch test for all 1+1 OLP circuits. Covers manual switch, OSNR verification on protect path, revert test, and acceptance log.
## Appendix E: Level 3 Architect Competency Sign-Off Checklist
This checklist is signed by the candidate and the certification panel lead upon successful completion of all 50 competencies. It is the official record of Level 3 Architect certification and is retained in the candidate's professional record.

| Item | Requirement | Verified | Panel Sign-Off |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | All 50 competencies: minimum 45 marked DEMONSTRATED | [ ] | _______________ |
| 2 | Part A (Written Exam): minimum score 70/100 | [ ] | _______________ |
| 3 | Part B (Live Simulation): minimum score 75%; all 6 faults resolved within 4 hours | [ ] | _______________ |
| 4 | Part C (Architecture Defense): minimum Acceptable rating in all 4 dimensions | [ ] | _______________ |
| 5 | Portfolio: minimum 8 complete, signed deliverables submitted | [ ] | _______________ |
| 6 | All module benchmark times documented; no more than 3 benchmarks exceeded by >50% | [ ] | _______________ |
| 7 | Capstone 6.4 (National Backbone Simulation) completed with all 5 deliverables | [ ] | _______________ |
| 8 | RCA report (Lab 7.2 or Capstone 6.4) reviewed and approved by panel | [ ] | _______________ |

**Candidate Name:** _________________________________    **Date:** _____________
**Panel Lead Signature:** _________________________________    **Certification #:** ___________
**Panel Member 2:** _________________________________
## Appendix F: Vendor TAC Escalation Guide
### Information to Have Ready Before Calling TAC

| Category | Required Information | Where to Find It |
|---|---|---|
| System Identification | Node ID, chassis serial number, software/firmware version, equipment CLEI/part number of affected card | show system identification or equivalent; equipment label |
| Alarm Data | Full alarm list (export or screenshot); alarm start timestamp; alarm severity and type | NMS alarm export; show alarm active CLI |
| PM Data | Current 15-min interval PM for all affected channels; historical PM for past 24–48 hours | NMS historian export; show channel pm history |
| Amplifier Status | Gain setpoint vs. actual; pump currents (A and B); NF estimate; gain tilt | show amplifier <id> status or equivalent |
| Optical Topology | Node location in route; span distances; adjacent node identities; fiber type | NMS topology view; network design documentation |
| Recent Changes | Any provisioning changes, firmware upgrades, maintenance activities in past 7 days | NMS change log; maintenance ticket system |
| Customer Impact | Number of affected circuits; SLA type; time of first impact; customer name (if TAC needs priority routing) | CRM/service management system |

### TAC Escalation Tiers

| Tier | Trigger Condition | Response Target | Escalation Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tier 1 (Standard) | Minor/Major alarm; no service impact; investigation in progress | 4-hour response | Open TAC case via portal; provide system ID and alarm data |
| Tier 2 (Priority) | Service degraded but protected; SLA breach risk within 8 hours; pump near MTBF | 2-hour response | Call TAC hotline + open portal case; mark as Priority; provide all PM and amplifier data |
| Tier 3 (Emergency) | Active service outage; SLA clock running; customer requesting escalation; data at risk | 15–30 minute acknowledgment | Call TAC emergency hotline immediately; quote circuit ID and customer name; request senior engineer assignment; provide all CLI outputs |
| Tier 4 (Executive) | Multi-customer impact; backbone failure; media risk; safety implications | Immediate (account team + VP-level) | TAC Tier 3 + escalate to account executive + engage vendor field SE; prepare for possible on-site vendor support |

1.title: Ch 1 — Optical Networking Landscape
1.content_md:
# Ch 1 — Optical Networking Landscape

## Evolution of Optical Transport

| Era | Technology | Capacity |
|---|---|---|
| 1980s | SONET/SDH | 2.5–10 Gbps |
| 1995–2005 | DWDM fixed-grid | 1.6–4 Tbps |
| 2010+ | Coherent + flex-grid ROADM | 20–96 Tbps |

## DWDM vs CWDM vs ROADM

| Feature | CWDM | DWDM | ROADM |
|---|---|---|---|
| Channel spacing | 20 nm | 50/100 GHz | Flexible |
| Channel count | 18 max | 96+ | 96+ |
| Amplification | Passive only | EDFA | EDFA + WSS |

1.content_md:
# Ch 2 — Fiber Fundamentals

## Span Loss

```
Span Loss = L × α + (n_conn × IL_conn) + (n_splice × IL_splice)
```

## Fiber Types

| ITU-T | Name | α (dB/km) | CD (ps/nm·km) |
|---|---|---|---|
| G.652D | Standard SMF | 0.20 | +17 |
| G.654E | Ultra-low loss | 0.16 | +21 |
| G.655C | NZDSF | 0.20 | +4 to +8 |

1.title: Ch 3 — DWDM Components
1.content_md:
# Ch 3 — DWDM Components

## EDFA Parameters

| Parameter | Typical | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Gain | 15–30 dB | Sets reach |
| Noise Figure | 4–6 dB | Limits OSNR |
| Output power | +17 to +23 dBm | Channel count limit |

## WSS (Wavelength Selective Switch)

Uses LCoS arrays to route individual wavelengths. Key specs: port count 1×9 to 1×20, insertion loss 4–7 dB, isolation >35 dB.

1.title: Ch 5 — Link Budget Calculation
1.content_md:
# Ch 5 — Link Budget Calculation

## Span Loss Formula

```
Span Loss = (L × α) + (n_conn × IL_conn) + margin
```

## OSNR at Receiver

```
OSNR = P_out − NF − 10·log10(h·f·Bref) − 10·log10(N_spans)
```

Minimum required: 14 dB (100G DP-QPSK), 18 dB (200G DP-16QAM).

| DP-64QAM | 12 | 28 dB | 200 km |

## FEC Types

- **CFEC**: 7% overhead, 8.5 dB NCG
- **EFEC**: 20% overhead, 11 dB NCG
- **OFEC**: OpenROADM standardized

1.title: Ch 7 — Network Topologies & Protection
1.content_md:
# Ch 7 — Network Topologies & Protection

| Topology | Resiliency | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| Point-to-Point | None | Low |
| Ring (OADM) | 1+1 auto | Medium |
| Mesh (ROADM) | Restoration | High |

## Protection Schemes

- **1+1 optical**: simultaneous Tx both paths, switch <50 ms
- **1:1 optical**: protection dark, switch <50 ms
- **GMPLS restoration**: route recalc, 200–500 ms

# Lab 1.1 — Architecture Technology Selection

## Scenario

120 km regional network, 8 wavelengths at 10G today, growing to 40 wavelengths in 3 years.

DWDM required: 120 km exceeds passive CWDM reach (~80 km), and 40-channel growth exceeds CWDM capacity.

2.title: Ch 2 — Advanced OSNR Engineering
2.content_md:
# Ch 2 — Advanced OSNR Engineering

## Full Budget Formula

```
OSNR = P_launch − NF − 10·log10(h·ν·B_ref) − 10·log10(N)
```

## 1200 km Example (16×75 km spans)

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Linear OSNR | 22.5 dB |
| NLI penalty | −1.5 dB |
| Net OSNR | **21.0 dB** |
| Required (200G) | 18 dB |
| Margin | **3.0 dB ✓** |

2.title: Ch 3 — Coherent Transmission Planning
2.content_md:
# Ch 3 — Coherent Transmission Planning

| Format | Line Rate | Reach |
|---|---|---|
| DP-QPSK | 100G | 3000+ km |
| DP-8QAM | 150G | 1800 km |
| DP-16QAM | 400G | 600 km |
| DP-64QAM | 800G | 120 km |

## Probabilistic Constellation Shaping (PCS)

Applies Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution, achieving 0.5–1.5 dB SNR gain.

## Rate-Adaptive Design

Modern transponders (Ciena WaveLogic 5, Nokia PSE-3) auto-select format based on real-time OSNR.
